domain: adapters
related: [adapters-react, adapters-vue, adapters-angular, grid-core, grid-features]
---

# Framework Adapters â€” Shared Mental Model

> Per-framework specifics live in [adapters-react.md](adapters-react.md), [adapters-vue.md](adapters-vue.md), [adapters-angular.md](adapters-angular.md).
>
> **DECIDED entries are facts + one-line WHY. History (what we tried, in what order) belongs in git log, not here.** When updating: condense, do not append.

## shared-architecture

- PATTERN: global singleton adapter registered at module load (React/Vue) or DI setup (Angular)
- FLOW: adapter registered â†’ grid parses light DOM â†’ user sets columns with framework components â†’ `processConfig()` wraps components in bridge fns â†’ grid calls renderer â†’ bridge mounts component into cell container
- INVARIANT: adapter MUST be registered before grid parses light DOM
- INVARIANT: one adapter per app (singleton)
- INTERFACE (all implement): `processConfig`, `createComponentRenderer`, `createComponentEditor`, `releaseCell`, plus 10 methods listed in `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` (see adapter-conformance)
- DECIDED (#330): `FrameworkAdapter.beginBatch?()` / `endBatch?()` defer per-cell teardown across bulk `releaseCell` loops (`_clearRowPool`, `renderVisibleRows` pool-shrink, `renderInlineRow` pre-wipe). Calls nest â€” outermost `endBatch` flushes. Listed in `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS`. Vue/Angular implement as no-ops (already batched).
- DECIDED (#338 / #340 reverted): adapter Context / `InjectionKey` / `InjectionToken` are plain module constants â€” MUST NOT be routed through `globalThis`. Rationale in [grid-core.md](grid-core.md) Â§ shared-store. Micro-frontend `customElements.define` collision belongs to #339.

## adapter-conformance

- OWNS: `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` in `libs/grid/src/lib/core/adapter-conformance.ts`, re-exported from `public.ts`
- INVARIANT: every adapter ships `<framework>-grid-adapter.conformance.spec.ts` that fails when any listed method is missing on instance (React/Vue) or prototype (Angular â€” ctor needs DI)
- INVARIANT: when core adds a hook it depends on, append to `CORE_CONSUMED_ADAPTER_METHODS` so all adapter conformance specs go red
- DECIDED: only the constant is exported from `public.ts`; helper fns stay internal. WHY: exporting them pushed `index.js` over budget. Each spec inlines a 2-line filter; DRY isn't worth the bytes.

## adapter-feature-purity (May 2026)

- DECIDED: adapter cores (`{react,vue,angular}-grid-adapter.ts`, `react-column-config.ts`) MUST NOT runtime-reference feature behaviour (type-only imports OK). Runtime wiring lives in `features/<name>.ts` (React/Vue) or `features/<name>/src/` (Angular) secondary entries via registered bridges/hooks. Mirrors WC plugin discipline.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 7): canonical cross-adapter registry surface re-exported from each adapter's primary `index.ts`. Symbol list + adapter-applicability lives in `.github/instructions/framework-adapters.instructions.md` ("Cross-adapter registry parity" bullet) â€” single source of truth. INVARIANT: additive-contract only (never remove/rename); when adding a registry to one adapter, mirror in same PR with identical export name + signature. Parity asserted by `*-grid-adapter.registry-parity.spec.ts` in each adapter (`typeof X === 'function'` per symbol; Angular spec also asserts React-only symbols are `undefined` to document the N/A intent). WHY: third-party features can target `@toolbox-web/grid-{react,vue,angular}` with the same import shape â€” no shell forks needed.

## config-entry-uniformity (component renderers via every entry point)

- INVARIANT: a framework component/JSX/VNode renderer inside `gridConfig.features.<name>` MUST be bridged to a vanilla DOM render fn identically to the per-feature prop/directive form. Same input â†’ same plugin, regardless of entry path. Tested per adapter.
- DECIDED: React/Vue bridge at the **feature factory** layer â€” `registerFeature(name, factory, { override: true })` (e.g. `features/grouping-columns.ts`) wraps the vanilla factory with JSX/VNodeâ†’DOM bridging. WHY uniform: core `grid.ts > #initializePlugins` â†’ `resolveFeatures` â†’ `createPluginsFromFeatures` â†’ `createPluginFromFeature` â†’ `getFeatureFactory(name)` is the SINGLE chokepoint core hits for every `config.features` entry, whether the config arrived via props (â†’ `plugins[]`) or via `gridConfig.features`. `processGridConfig`(React)/`mergedConfig`(Vue) spread `features` through untouched, so the JSX fn reaches the override factory unmodified. Test: `features/grouping-columns.spec.tsx` exercises the factory directly.
- DECIDED: Angular bridges at the **preprocessor** layer (`registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor`, bridges Component/TemplateRef), NOT a factory override â€” so the preprocessor must run on BOTH entry paths. `createFeaturePlugins` (directive path) ran it; the `gridConfig.features` path (the `set gridConfig` â†’ `processConfig` interceptor) did NOT until the June 2026 fix added a `getFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name)` loop over `gridConfig.features.*`. SYMPTOM before fix: `TypeError: Class constructor â€¦ cannot be invoked without 'new'` at afterRender (raw class reached the core plugin factory). Tests: `angular-grid-adapter.spec.ts` "processGridConfig / processConfig" feature-bridging cases. See [adapters-angular.md](adapters-angular.md).
- DOCS: all framework tabs lead with `gridConfig.features` (uniform best practice); per-feature prop/directive/light-dom is shown as an "Alternative" note, never removed.

## shell-content-wrappers (#352)

- OWNS: React `Grid{Header,Toolbar}Content`, Vue `TbwGrid{Header,Toolbar}Content`, Angular `Grid{Header,Toolbar}Content` directives â€” wrap `registerHeaderContent`/`registerToolbarContent` (specs co-located). Vue uses built-in `<Teleport>` (slot child of `<TbwGrid>`); Angular uses `host.closest('tbw-grid')` (NOT `injectGrid()` â€” descendants).
- INVARIANT: render-callback cleanup is a **no-op** AND `register*` MUST `await grid.ready?.()` then guard unmount-during-await (React `unmounted` / Vue `cancelled` / Angular `destroyed`).
- INVARIANT (Angular extra): render MUST be idempotent (`createEmbeddedView` is not â€” skip when `viewRef.rootNodes[0]` still inside container, else destroy stale viewRef first). WHY: grid reuses SAME container by id across shell refreshes (`shell.ts` cleans â†’ re-renders same node); real teardown destroys child state; await without flag leaks registration.
- DECIDED (#370, React + Vue + Angular): wrappers route register/unregister through `grid.getPluginByName?.('shell')?.{register,unregister}{Header,Toolbar}Content`. **v3: the deprecated grid-element delegate fallback (`grid.register*Content`) is REMOVED** â€” those delegates no longer exist on the grid element (TS2339), so the wrappers keep ONLY the `shell?.*` plugin path. Typing: bare `import type { HeaderContentDefinition | ToolbarContentDefinition } from '@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/shell'` (NOT the package root â€” the root no longer exports them) also activates the `PluginNameMap` augmentation so `getPluginByName('shell')` is typed â€” no cast. Files: `grid-react/src/lib/grid-{header,toolbar}-content.tsx`, `grid-vue/src/lib/TbwGrid{Header,Toolbar}Content.vue`, `grid-angular/src/lib/directives/grid-{header,toolbar}-content.directive.ts`; tests `*ShellContent*.spec.* > shell-plugin routing (#370)`. `GridToolPanel`/`TbwGridToolPanel` need no change (render light-DOM `<tbw-grid-tool-panel>`, shell parses it).
- DECIDED (#370): adapter-side shell-plugin auto-registration DEFERRED to v3. WHY: core unconditionally auto-registers `ShellPlugin` at index 0 in `grid.ts` `#initializePlugins` (v2.x) unless a `shell`-named plugin already resolved, so an adapter injecting `new ShellPlugin()` is inert dead code in v2.x (core dedups by name) that only adds a runtime ShellPlugin dependency + untestable branches. Revisit when core stops auto-registering (v3).
- DECIDED (v3 enablement): each adapter now ships an opt-in `features/shell` side-effect import (`@toolbox-web/grid-{react,vue,angular}/features/shell` â†’ body `import '@toolbox-web/grid/features/shell'`). Wiring mirrors any other feature subpath: React/Vue add `'features/shell'` to `vite.config.mts` `build.lib.entry` + `import './shell'` to `src/features/index.ts`; Angular adds folder `features/shell/{ng-package.json,src/index.ts}` (auto-discovered, NO directive â€” shell is config-driven via `gridConfig.features.shell`/shell-content wrappers). ALL THREE `internal/feature-augmentations.ts` get `_ShellAugmentation`. GOTCHA: once `shell` is a global `FeatureConfig` key in the adapter tsc program, BOTH React/Vue drift guards fire â€” (a) `feature-props.ts` `_AssertFeaturePropsCoverCore` needs `shell?: boolean | ShellConfig` (import `ShellConfig` from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/shell`); (b) `feature-prop-keys.ts` `_AssertBuiltinCoversCore` needs `'shell'` in `_KnownBuiltinGaps` (NOT `BUILTIN_FEATURE_PROP_KEYS`) â€” shell is typed-but-not-runtime-extracted (config-driven), like the stickyRows/rowDragDrop gaps. Angular has no feature-props guard. Registration verified by `features/shell{.spec.ts,/src/index.spec.ts}` (`createPluginFromFeature('shell', true).name === 'shell'`). Builds emit `dist/.../features/shell.js` (~0.04â€“0.08 kB).
- DECIDED (#370, Phase 3.5 dogfood): core-based demos/docs migrated to the shell **feature** API â€” `import '@toolbox-web/grid/features/shell'` + `features: { shell }` (not top-level `config.shell`, not `plugins: [new ShellPlugin()]`); imperative registration via `grid.getPluginByName('shell')?.register{ToolPanel,HeaderContent,ToolbarContent}`. Files: `ShellBasicDemo`/`ShellMultiPanelsDemo`/`ShellLightDomDemo`/`ShellToolbarButtonsDemo`/`ThemeBuilder.astro`, `demos/vanilla/.../{employee-management/tool-panels.ts,calendar,booking-logs}`. GOTCHA: `ToolPanelDefinition.title` is OPTIONAL as of #430 (both the plugin `registerToolPanel` API and the light-DOM `<tbw-grid-tool-panel>` parser accept a missing title â€” a title-less single panel skips its accordion header row). Adapter title inputs match: React `GridToolPanelProps.title?`, Angular `GridToolPanel.title = input<string>()`, Vue `title?` (resolves to `''`). `config.shell` & `refreshShellHeader()` do NOT warn TBW076 (config merged cleanly; only the 15 grid-element delegates warn) â€” config migration is best-practice, not warning-driven.
- DECIDED (#260/#261/#262, v3): core stopped auto-registering ShellPlugin (`grid.ts` `#initializePlugins` â€” shell resolves ONLY from `features:{shell}` or `plugins:[ShellPlugin]`). Top-level `config.shell` alone NO LONGER enables shell (it's only merged by `ShellPlugin.processConfig` AFTER the plugin instantiates). Consequence: **adapter** employee/calendar demos now `import '@toolbox-web/grid-{react,vue,angular}/features/shell'` AND move shell config under `features: { shell: {...} }` (mirrors every other feature: import registers the factory, `features.shell` truthy enables it). WHY no library magic: user-confirmed "shell configured the same way any other feature/plugin is â€” imported, then added to gridConfig" (NOT component-coupled auto-register). A child-feature detector returning `{ shell: true }` would be INSUFFICIENT â€” core drops unregistered feature names (TBW031); the factory must be imported. Files: `demos/{react,vue,angular}/src/demos/{employee-management/grid-config.ts,calendar/*}`.
- DECIDED (#260/#261/#262, v3 â€” spec harnesses): shell-content `*ShellContent*.spec.*` behavioral harnesses route register/unregister through a **mock shell plugin** via `grid.getPluginByName('shell')`, NOT grid-element delegate methods (removed in v3). Angular reuses the same spies on both the element and the shell object so existing `grid.registerHeaderContent`/`grid.headerDefs` assertions hold; React/Vue expose a `shell` field on the harness. The `#370` routing tests already used this; only the older delegate-based behavioral tests needed migrating.
- DECIDED (#260/#261/#262, v3 â€” other core removals mirrored): (a) `@toolbox-web/grid/features/reorder-rows` module DELETED â†’ all three adapters dropped the `reorderRows` feature prop/`@Input`, `RowReorderConfig` import, `_ReorderRowsAugmentation`, the `features/reorder-rows` secondary entry (React/Vue: `features/reorder-rows.ts` + `vite.config.mts` entry + `features/index.ts` import; Angular: `features/reorder-rows/` folder + `typedoc.json` line; React/Vue also `BUILTIN_FEATURE_PROP_KEYS`). (b) `PinnedRowsConfig.customPanels` removed from core â†’ adapter `PinnedRowsConfig` widening dropped the `customPanels` member (now `Omit<CorePinnedRowsConfig,'slots'> & { slots?: PinnedRowSlot[] }`); pinned-rows feature bridges only `slots[]`; customPanels specs removed. (c) shell type aliases `HeaderContentDefinition`/`ToolbarContentDefinition` now import from `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/shell` (root no longer exports them).

## bridge-registries (append-only, populated by side-effect imports)

| Registry                                                                       | Module                                                                                   | Installed by                                                                                            | Purpose                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `registerEditorMountHook` / `notifyEditorMounted`                              | `editor-mount-hooks.ts` (each adapter)                                                   | `features/editing`                                                                                      | `before-edit-close` â†’ native `.blur()` on focused input                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| `registerFilterPanelTypeDefaultBridge`                                         | each adapter                                                                             | `features/filtering`                                                                                    | wraps user `filterPanelRenderer` in framework render path                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `registerDetailRendererBridge` / `registerResponsiveCardRendererBridge`        | `react-grid-adapter.ts` / `vue-grid-adapter.ts` / `angular: internal/feature-bridges.ts` | `features/master-detail` / `features/responsive`                                                        | turns user template/component into renderer fn; adapter method becomes thin delegate                                                                                                                                                        |
| `registerPostMountRefresh(name, fn)` / `notifyPostMount`                       | `post-mount-refresh-hooks.ts` (React + Vue)                                              | `features/master-detail`, `features/responsive`                                                         | feature-keyed kick fired after framework children commit so plugins created pre-children pick up renderers. Angular N/A â€” `ngAfterContentInit` covers this imperatively                                                                     |
| `registerChildFeatureDetector(name, fn)` / `detectChildFeatures`               | `child-feature-detector.ts` (React only)                                                 | `data-grid.tsx` pre-registers built-ins at module load; third-party features may add more               | scans React children by component `displayName`, returns partial `FeatureProps` to auto-enable plugins. Vue uses slots for templates not feature signals; Angular uses attribute-selector directives â€” neither needs a scan                 |
| `registerFeaturePropKey(name)` / `getFeaturePropKeys`                          | `feature-prop-keys.ts` (React + Vue)                                                     | module-load self-registration of every built-in key; third-party feature secondary entries may add more | canonical list of `FeatureProps` keys `<DataGrid>` / `<TbwGrid>` extract from `...rest` / declared props before calling `createPluginsFromFeatures`. Angular N/A â€” per-feature `@Input()` directives, no spread, nothing to discriminate    |
| `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name, fn)` / `getFeatureConfigPreprocessor` | `internal/feature-extensions.ts` (Angular only)                                          | `features/grouping-columns`, `grouping-rows`, `pinned-rows`, `master-detail`, `responsive`, `filtering` | bridges Angular `TemplateRef`/`Component` class shapes embedded in feature config to core's renderer fn. Invoked uniformly by `Grid.createFeaturePlugins` for every feature. React/Vue N/A â€” JSX nodes and VNodes are portal/teleport-ready |
| `registerTemplateBridge` (Angular)                                             | `internal/feature-extensions.ts`                                                         | `features/master-detail`, `responsive`                                                                  | wires `<tbw-grid-detail>`/`<tbw-grid-responsive-card>` templates in `ngAfterContentInit`                                                                                                                                                    |

- INVARIANT: features import from the adapter's primary entry (registry hooks + types); adapter primary entry MUST NOT import from feature secondary entries (build cycle).
- INVARIANT: spec files exercising bridge behaviour MUST add side-effect imports of `'../features/<name>'` at the top, mirroring real consumers. Otherwise the bridge is null and methods return `undefined`.
- DECIDED: `createToolPanelRenderer` stays on adapter (CORE primitive â€” `shell.ts` parses `<tbw-grid-tool-panel>` and calls it). `createEditor`/`createRenderer` stay on adapter (per-cell primitives, not editing-feature-specific). `createFilterPanelRenderer` stays inline (generic node-to-container wrap; only filter-feature dep is type-only).
- DECIDED (#354): adapter `pinned-rows` slot-renderer wrappers MUST cache the host element per renderer across calls. WHY: `PinnedRowsPlugin.renderPanelSlot` (`libs/grid/src/lib/plugins/pinned-rows/pinned-rows.ts` L432-510) reference-checks renderer outputs in `rowOutputsCache` (WeakMap) to skip row rebuild; fresh host each call triggers React/Vue unmount-remount and Angular re-create. Pattern: React/Vue closure-cache `{host, portalKey}` â†’ `renderToContainer(host, node, key)` for in-place update + `PinnedRowsPluginWithCleanup` subclass overrides `detach()` to `removeFromContainer`; Angular closure-caches `{hostElement, componentRef}` â†’ `setInput(name, value) + detectChanges()`, relies on adapter `componentRefs[]` destroyed at `dispose()`. Specs: `libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/features/pinned-rows.spec.{tsx,ts}` (`expect(second).toBe(first)` + detach), `libs/grid-angular/features/pinned-rows/src/cached-panel-renderer.spec.ts` (pure helper, extracted from `index.ts` to avoid DI mocking).
- DECIDED (#353, retroactively extended): feature configs that carry renderer callbacks expose adapter-typed wrappers so consumers can return framework-native nodes. Pattern: `Omit<CoreConfig, 'rendererProp'> & { rendererProp?: CoreConfig['rendererProp'] | FrameworkReturn }`. ADAPTER TYPE NAMING POLICY: adapter feature-props re-export the extended type under the SAME canonical name as the core type using a `Core*` import alias (mirrors `GridConfig`/`DataGridElement` precedent â€” consumers see one name across vanilla + framework). Applies to ALL widening types across all three adapters: React `lib/feature-props.ts` exposes `FilterConfig`, `ColumnGroupDefinition`, `GroupingColumnsConfig`, `GroupingRowsConfig`, `PanelRender`/`PanelSlot`/`PinnedRowSlot`/`PinnedRowsConfig`/`ZonedPanelRender`, `MasterDetailConfig`, `ResponsivePluginConfig`; Vue mirrors with `VNode`; Angular per-feature directives expose them widened to `| Type<unknown>` (also `FilterConfig` for plugin-level `filterPanelRenderer`). The legacy framework-prefixed names (`React*`, `Vue*`, `Angular*`) were REMOVED in the 3.0 deprecation cleanup â€” only the canonical unprefixed names are exported (specs may still alias locally via `import { PinnedRowsConfig as ReactPinnedRowsConfig }`). RUNTIME BRIDGING: React/Vue use `createPluginFromFeature` factory override (subclass `*PluginWithCleanup` tracks portal/teleport keys and overrides `detach()` to call `removeFromContainer(key)`); Angular uses `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor` + `adapter.mountComponentRenderer`. Specs: `libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/features/{master-detail,responsive,grouping-{columns,rows},pinned-rows}.spec.{tsx,ts}` (`expect(out).toBeInstanceOf(HTMLElement)` + detach), `libs/grid-angular/features/{master-detail,responsive,filtering,grouping-{columns,rows},pinned-rows}/src/{index,*-bridge}.spec.ts`. INVARIANT: spec assertions read `plugin.userConfig`, NOT `plugin.config` â€” `config` is only populated post-`attach()` (see `base-plugin.ts` line 597). INVARIANT: Angular spec files must `import '@angular/compiler';` at the top before importing the side-effect feature module, otherwise `@angular/forms` transitively triggers `JIT compilation failed for PlatformLocation`. INVARIANT: when an adapter feature file (e.g. `features/pinned-rows.ts`, `features/grouping-columns/src/index.ts`) imports core types AND re-exports the adapter-widened version under the same name, alias the core import with `Core*` to avoid local-binding collisions.

## adapter-event-props (drift-safety pattern, cross-cutting)

- OWNS (React): `EventProps<TRow>` + `EVENT_PROP_MAP` in `libs/grid-react/src/lib/event-props.ts`. Guard: `} as const satisfies Readonly<Record<keyof EventProps, keyof DataGridEventMap<unknown>>>;` (bidirectional).
- OWNS (Angular): `output<T>()` + `eventOutputMap` in `directives/grid.directive.ts`. Guard: `} as const satisfies Readonly<Record<string, keyof DataGridEventMap<unknown>>>;` (value-side only). Forward-only complement: `_assertEventOutputMapCoversCore` Exclude-extends-never type. Widen `_intentionallyOmittedEvents` to consciously skip.
- INVARIANT: NEVER widen `satisfies` to silence a complaint. The complaint is the feature: either drop the prop OR add the missing `declare module '../../core/types'` augmentation.
- INVARIANT: when satisfies rejects an event, check emit sites (`grep '_emit(' / 'event-name'`) â€” plugins emit + registry can diverge silently.
- INVARIANT: adding a `DataGridEventMap` entry wires all three: React `EVENT_PROP_MAP` + `EventProps`, Vue `EVENT_MAP` + `defineEmits`, Angular `eventOutputMap` + `output<T>()`. Vue has NO drift guard yet.
- INVARIANT: missing core public exports surface as Angular build failure (not Vue/React). Angular `@toolbox-web/grid/all` deep-import only sees `libs/grid/src/public.ts` â€” new event detail types must be re-exported there.
- DECIDED: `EventProps` is flat (one entry per event, even paired `onUndo`/`onRedo`). Tried `onUndoRedo` â€” bound to non-emitted `'undo-redo'`.
- DECIDED: hand-maintained interface + `satisfies` (not mapped type â€” TypeDoc renders as `unknown`).
- TENSION (Angular): `eventOutputMap` keys not statically tied to `output<T>()` fields. Forgotten entry = silent runtime bug.

## event-handling â€” `before-edit-close` blur bridge

- DECIDED: editor `before-edit-close` â†’ native `.blur()` on focused input/textarea/select. WHY: Tab + programmatic row exits rebuild cell DOM synchronously; `onBlur={commit}` / `@blur="commit"` / `(blur)="commit()"` would lose pending input. Native `.blur()` (NOT synthetic `FocusEvent`) â€” React 17+ delegates focus via bubbling `focusout`. Angular parallel: `BaseGridEditor.onBeforeEditClose()`.
- DECIDED (#249): bridge wires BOTH slot/JSX `createEditor` path AND config-path wrappers: React `wrapReactEditor`; Vue `createConfigVNodeEditor`/`createConfigComponentEditor`/`createTypeEditor`; Angular `createComponentEditor` + template `createEditor`. Tab from last cell â†’ `#exitRowEdit(currentRow,false)` â†’ `beginBulkEdit(nextRow)` synchronously â€” focused input never blurs naturally. Grid resolved lazily via `queueMicrotask`.
- CLEANUP: React threads `unsub` through `mountedPortals` (`cleanupConfigRootsIn`); Vue/Angular store in `editorMountTeardowns` Map (container/host-keyed), swept in `releaseCell` + `destroy()`.
- DECIDED (Tier 1): install moved out of adapter into `editor-mount-hooks.ts` notifier. `attachBeforeEditCloseFlush` â†’ `runEditorMountHooks`. `features/editing` registers.
- TESTS: `react-column-config.spec.ts > flushes the focused input on before-edit-close`; `vue-grid-adapter.spec.ts > flushes the focused input on before-edit-close for config-based VNode editor`; `angular-grid-adapter.spec.ts > before-edit-close blur bridge`.

## event-handling â€” `releaseCell` DOM-recycle invariant (#250)

- DECIDED: core MUST call `grid.__frameworkAdapter?.releaseCell?.(cell)` BEFORE any sync wipe (`innerHTML=''` / `replaceChildren()`) of a cell that may contain adapter-managed renderer DOM. Without it framework's tree references orphans and next commit throws `NotFoundError: removeChild`.
- COVERED PATHS: `editor-injection.ts` (open editor); `renderVisibleRows` pool-shrink (`core/internal/rows.ts`); `_clearRowPool` (`grid.ts`). All guarded by `cell.firstElementChild`.
- KNOWN WEAKNESSES: `GroupingRowsPlugin.ts` and `pinned-rows.ts` have `rowEl.innerHTML=''` paths that do NOT call `releaseCell` first (rely on PortalBoundary / equivalent).
- TESTS: `rows.spec.ts > releases cells when pool shrinks`; per-adapter spec coverage in `adapters-react.md` / `adapters-vue.md` / `adapters-angular.md`.

## feature-prop-bridging

- All adapters expose feature props that auto-load plugins. React: `<DataGrid selection="range" editing="dblclick" />`. Vue: `<TbwGrid :selection="'range'" :editing="'dblclick'" />`. Angular: `<tbw-grid [selection]="'range'" [editing]="'dblclick'">`.
- MECHANISM: feature props â†’ `createPluginsFromFeatures()` â†’ factory creates plugins â†’ added to `gridConfig.plugins`.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 1): React/Vue `FeatureName = Exclude<keyof FeatureConfig, '__brand'>` (derived from core's augmentable interface â€” third-party features auto-join; `__brand` sentinel filtered out). Strict-additive enforced by `feature-registry.spec.ts` in all three adapters. Angular followed in phase 6.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 2): per-feature `registerPostMountRefresh(name, fn)` registry replaces hard-coded plugin refresh calls in `data-grid.tsx` / `TbwGrid.vue`. WHY: shell can't grow per-feature knowledge for every future "refresh after children commit" need. Map-keyed (re-register replaces â€” HMR-friendly). React + Vue only â€” Angular's `ngAfterContentInit` lifecycle hook covers this imperatively; no registry needed. Tests: `post-mount-refresh-hooks.spec.ts` in React + Vue.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 3): `registerChildFeatureDetector(displayName, fn)` registry â€” React only. Replaces the inline `displayName === 'GridDetailPanel' | 'GridResponsiveCard'` scan in `data-grid.tsx`. Built-in detectors are PRE-REGISTERED at `data-grid.tsx` module load (NOT via feature side-effect imports) â€” preserves existing behaviour for users who render `<GridDetailPanel>` without importing `features/master-detail` (gh #356 Â§7 #4: no changed default behaviour). Third-party React features add their own via the registry. Vue uses default slots for templates not feature signals; Angular uses attribute-selector directives â€” neither needs the scan. Tests: `child-feature-detector.spec.ts` in React.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 4): `registerFeaturePropKey(name)` / `getFeaturePropKeys()` registry â€” React + Vue. Replaces the hand-maintained `FEATURE_KEYS`/`FEATURE_PROPS` array. Pre-populated at module load with every built-in name. Known pre-existing gaps kept as-is: Vue BUILTIN omits `rowDragDrop`; BOTH BUILTIN lists omit `stickyRows`. Compile-time drift guard `_AssertBuiltinCoversCore` in each `feature-prop-keys.ts` enforces `BUILTIN âŠ‡ keyof FeatureConfig` minus `_KnownBuiltinGaps` allowlist. `Set<FeatureName>` storage with a cached frozen-array snapshot rebuilt lazily on mutation (`<DataGrid>` / `<TbwGrid>` call `getFeaturePropKeys()` on every render/build). Angular N/A â€” per-feature `@Input()` directives, no `{...rest}` spread, nothing to discriminate. Tests: `feature-prop-keys.spec.ts` in React + Vue.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 5, extended May 2026): `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name, fn)` / `getFeatureConfigPreprocessor(name)` â€” Angular only (`libs/grid-angular/src/lib/internal/feature-extensions.ts`). Invoked uniformly by `Grid.createFeaturePlugins` at `directives/grid.directive.ts:~1695` for EVERY feature (no per-feature plumbing on the adapter). WHY Angular-only: Angular feature configs carry `TemplateRef`/`Component` class shapes that must be bridged to core's renderer functions; React/Vue use a `createPluginFromFeature` factory override instead (JSX/VNode bridged inside the plugin subclass). Used by: `pinned-rows`, `grouping-rows`, `grouping-columns`, `master-detail`, `responsive`, `filtering` (plugin-level `filterPanelRenderer`). Per-feature specs co-located.
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 6): Angular `FeatureName` flipped from a hand-listed 25-entry union to `Exclude<keyof FeatureConfig, '__brand'>` (parity with React/Vue, gh #356 Â§5). WHY: ng-packagr partial compilation needed every `@toolbox-web/grid/features/*` module on its type graph at emit time to see the `FeatureConfig` augmentations. SOLUTION: types-only barrel `libs/grid-angular/src/lib/internal/feature-augmentations.ts` with one `import type {} from '@toolbox-web/grid/features/<name>'` per feature; `feature-registry.ts` does `import './internal/feature-augmentations'` (BARE side-effect import â€” NOT `import type {} from`; tsc preserves bare imports through emit so ng-packagr's rollup-of-dts sees the augmenting modules). Symptom of getting this wrong: `nx build grid-angular` fails with `TS2345: Argument of type '"selection"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'never'` in `grid.directive.ts` (FeatureName collapsed to `never`). Verified: `dist/libs/grid-angular/types/toolbox-web-grid-angular.d.ts` emits `type FeatureName = Exclude<keyof FeatureConfig, '__brand'>`. Also fixed React's previously dead drift guard (`_AssertFeaturePropsCoverCore`) by adding the `'__brand'` exclusion + `const _featurePropsCoverCore: ... = true` runtime anchor, and mirrored the guard in Vue's `feature-props.ts`. Tests: `feature-registry.spec.ts` superset assertion (all 3 adapters).
- DECIDED (May 2026, typedoc fix): React/Vue MIRROR the Angular barrel â€” `libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/lib/internal/feature-augmentations.ts` with one `export type { _Augmentation as _<Name>Augmentation } from '@toolbox-web/grid/features/<name>'` per feature; `feature-registry.ts` pulls it via `import './internal/feature-augmentations'`. WHY: `bun nx build` uses Vite + esbuild (transpile-only, no full typecheck) so the missing augmentations never surfaced, but `bun nx typedoc` runs `tsc --strict` over an entry-point-only program (`src/index.ts` + a few feature entries) and `keyof FeatureConfig` collapses to whatever entry points transitively import â€” failing with `TS2322: Type '"filtering"' is not assignable to type 'FeatureName'` for any name not on a transitive path. The barrel is the single fix that keeps `FeatureName` honest under any tool that builds its own TS program (typedoc, ng-packagr, future strict typecheck targets). INVARIANT: any new core feature MUST be appended to ALL THREE `internal/feature-augmentations.ts` files in the same PR. Tests: `bun nx run-many -t typedoc -p grid-react,grid-vue,grid-angular`.
- DECIDED (Jun 2026): Adapter `features/<name>.ts` modules that DON'T re-register (pure delegators) and modules that override the factory MUST still surface the core `FeatureConfig` augmentation to a CONSUMER who imports only that subpath. Two emit-survival facts: (1) a BARE `import '@toolbox-web/grid/features/<name>'` is STRIPPED from the emitted `.d.ts` by vite-plugin-dts (confirmed: `selection.d.ts` loses it) â€” so it works only when the consumer path-maps to SOURCE, not against published dist; (2) a NAMED `export type { _Augmentation as _<Name>Augmentation } from '@toolbox-web/grid/features/<name>'` SURVIVES emit and drags the augmenting module onto the consumer's type graph. `features/filtering.ts` (React + Vue) overrides the factory with a portal/teleport-aware version and imported only `@toolbox-web/grid/plugins/filtering` (the PLUGIN), never the feature â€” so `filtering?` was missing from `FeatureConfig` even in SOURCE â†’ consumer `features: { filtering: ... }` failed TS2353. FIX: every `libs/grid-{react,vue}/src/features/*.ts` subpath now carries a named `export type { _Augmentation as _<Name>Augmentation } from '@toolbox-web/grid/features/<name>'` (delegators keep their bare runtime import too; grouping-columns/grouping-rows re-export from the core `features/grouping-{columns,rows}` subpath, NOT the plugin). Runtime unaffected (type-only re-export = 0 runtime; override factories need no prior core registration). Verified: build emits the re-export into every `dist/libs/grid-{react,vue}/features/*.d.ts` (0 missing); scratch consumer path-mapped to `dist/` importing ONLY subpaths (`tooltip`/`visibility`/`grouping-columns`/`master-detail` React, `tooltip`/`selection` Vue) type-checks clean â€” `selection: true` correctly errors TS2322 (value mismatch), proving the prop is RECOGNIZED, not TS2353. INVARIANT: any NEW adapter feature subpath MUST include the named `_<Name>Augmentation` re-export from its core `features/<name>` subpath, or published-dist consumers importing only that subpath hit TS2353. The `internal/feature-augmentations.ts` barrel does NOT rescue dist consumers (its bare import is stripped from `feature-registry.d.ts`). Angular already does both (bare import + named re-export per feature; ng-packagr preserves bare imports anyway).
- DECIDED (gh #356 phase 7, cleanup): Each adapter ships ONLY the registries its shell actually invokes. RULED OUT: "ship-without-caller for API parity" â€” if the shell doesn't call it, it's a stable third-party API for nothing (dead code). Final per-adapter map: React = `registerEditorMountHook`, `registerPostMountRefresh`, `registerChildFeatureDetector`, `registerFeaturePropKey`. Vue = `registerEditorMountHook`, `registerPostMountRefresh`, `registerFeaturePropKey`. Angular = `registerEditorMountHook`, `registerFeatureConfigPreprocessor`/`getFeatureConfigPreprocessor`. Asymmetry is framework-shaped (lifecycle hooks, child-discovery patterns, component-system shapes), not historical drift. Deleted modules: React `feature-config-preprocessors.ts` + spec; Vue `child-feature-detector.ts` + spec, `feature-config-preprocessors.ts` + spec; Angular `post-mount-refresh-hooks.ts` + spec. Parity specs (`<adapter>-grid-adapter.registry-parity.spec.ts`) rewritten with explicit positive + negative assertions per adapter. Tests: full adapter suites green (test+build+lint on grid-react, grid-vue, grid-angular).

## three-way-parity (May 2026 audit)

- DECIDED: React/Vue/Angular aligned â€” 26 feature props, 28 grid events, hooks/composables, providers, tree-shake registration, bridges, registries.
- DECIDED (#289): cell/editor context generics use asymmetric defaults `TRow = unknown`, `TValue = any` (Vue `CellSlotProps`/`EditorSlotProps`/`GridCellContext`/`GridEditorContext`; React `GridCellContext`/`GridEditorContext`). WHY: `unknown` for row safety, `any` for `TValue` unblocks single-generic `CellSlotProps<MyRow>` (narrowing awkward in templates). Tests: `slot-types.spec.ts`, `context-types.spec.ts`.
- DECIDED (#438, nested dotted-path fields): all 3 adapters inherit nested-path `field` support automatically (their `ColumnConfig` `Omit`s only renderers, so `field` flows from core). Each adapter `ColumnConfig`/`GridConfig` gained a defaulted `TField extends string = ColumnFieldKey<TRow>` generic so strict opt-in (`NestedPaths<TRow>`) composes WITH adapter renderers â€” `GridConfig<Deal, NestedPaths<Deal>>`. GOTCHA: adapters alias core `ColumnConfig` as `BaseColumnConfig` â€” core `ColumnConfig<TRow, TField>` has only **2** params (not 3 like core `BaseColumnConfig<TRow, TValue, TField>`), so adapter `extends Omit<BaseColumnConfig<TRow, TField>, â€¦>` (NOT `<TRow, any, TField>` â€” ng-packagr fails `TS2707`). `ColumnFieldKey`/`NestedPaths` re-exported from all 3 `index.ts`. Context `field` types are all `string` (React `GridCellContext`/`GridEditorContext` widened `keyof TRow & string`â†’`string`; Vue `EditorSlotProps.field` already `string`, `CellSlotProps` has none; Angular `GridCellContext` has none). Tests: `nested-field-parity.spec.ts` in each adapter.
- INVARIANT: public export added to one adapter â†’ mirror in same PR. `index.ts` = canonical surface.
- INVARIANT: `AllFeatureProps<TRow>` shape identical React/Vue. If `SSRProps` truly removed, drop atomically (in the same coordinated release).
- DECIDED (v3, #261/#262): React (#261) AND Vue (#262) both dropped `SSRProps`/`ssr` â€” `AllFeatureProps<TRow> = FeatureProps<TRow>` in both. React also removed the `ssr` destructure + `featurePlugins` skip in `data-grid.tsx`; Vue never wired `ssr` into plugin creation (`TbwGrid.vue` iterates `getFeaturePropKeys()`), so its removal was surface-only. React/Vue `AllFeatureProps` parity restored. Vue #262 also removed the deprecated `makeFlushFocusedInput` re-export from `vue-grid-adapter.ts` (real callers import from `./editor-mount-hooks`).
- TENSION: idiomatic differences are NOT parity gaps (`<DataGrid>` vs `<TbwGrid>`, render props vs slots, `GridElementContext` vs `GRID_ELEMENT_KEY`, `PortalManager` vs `TeleportManager`).
- HISTORICAL GAPS (now closed): Vue/Angular were missing `cellCancel`, `editOpen`, `beforeEditClose`, `editClose`, `dirtyChange`, `dataChange`, `columnResizeReset`, `groupExpand`, `groupCollapse`, `contextMenuOpen`. Vue's `'undo-redo'` was latent bug â€” split into `'undo'`/`'redo'`. Angular gained `column-shorthand.ts` mirror, `cardRowHeight` on `GridResponsiveCard`, `getPlugin<T>(cls)` + `getPluginByName(name)` on `injectGrid()`, `provideGrid({typeDefaults, icons})`, event-output drift guard.

## cross-adapter-tensions

- REGISTRATION TIMING: React/Vue auto-register at module load (sync); Angular requires explicit DI setup
- WEAKMAP FALLBACK: dual lookup (WeakMap â†’ field name â†’ factory) needed because framework re-renders create new DOM elements
- PORTAL/TELEPORT OVERHEAD: mounting framework components into web component cells requires bridge layer managing lifecycle outside framework's component tree

## adapter-conformance-quick-table

| Framework | Registration           | Event idiom                                                                | Programmatic access                         |
| --------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| React     | global at module load  | props `onCellClick={(d)=>...}` (event-props.ts maps to `addEventListener`) | `useGrid<TRow>()` hook                      |
| Vue       | global at module load  | emits `@cell-click="handler"` (props â†’ `addEventListener`)                 | `useGrid<TRow>()` composable (Ref wrappers) |
| Angular   | DI via `provideGrid()` | outputs `(cellClick)="handler($event)"` (directive wires native â†’ outputs) | `injectGrid()`                              |
